assets.name: lecture_06_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Among these very similar frames, candidate 04 shows the small polarization mark in its most finished state and with the least hand occlusion. The board content is sparse, but this frame preserves the notation clearly enough to support a careful redraw for the notes.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: draw double arrows on the polarization.

assets.name: lecture_06_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 is the clearest and tightest crop of the blackboard content. It preserves both the bra-ket orthogonality statement and the matrix-column-vector product without lecturer occlusion, and the slightly closer framing makes the notation more legible for note writing than the earlier candidates.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: That matrix times that column vector,

assets.name: lecture_06_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 gives a clean, fully unobstructed view of the completed sketch. The two peaks and the connecting baseline are clearly visible, and unlike candidate 04 there is no hand entering the frame. It is the best evidentiary screenshot for a nearby clean redraw.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: There's a wave function which is nice and symmetric.
